headline: GOLD DROPS TO $4,571 AS HIGHER YIELDS AND FADING FEAR WEIGH SHORT-TERM; $5,400 YEAR-END TARGETS INTACT
snippet: Gold fell to $4,571 on May 1 as rising Treasury yields and a diminishing geopolitical fear premium triggered a pullback from recent highs, ending a multi-week run that briefly pushed the metal toward $5,000. Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan maintain year-end targets of $5,400 and $5,055 respectively, arguing the structural floor — central bank buying at ~60 tonnes per month and persistent inflation — remains intact.

headline: B.C. FAST-TRACKS KSM AND NEW INGERBELLE AS PROVINCE SWELLS PRIORITY PROJECTS LIST TO 35
snippet: British Columbia added 17 projects to its provincial priority list — bringing the total to 35 — with new inclusions covering Seabridge Gold's KSM copper-gold project and the New Ingerbelle extension at Hudbay Minerals' Copper Mountain mine. The designations unlock expedited environmental review timelines and mark a meaningful shift in B.C.'s permitting posture toward large-scale mining development.

headline: VISIBLE GOLD RAISES C$2.6M TO DRILL THE DOORSTEP OF IAMGOLD'S 11-MILLION-OZ NELLIGAN COMPLEX IN QUÉBEC
snippet: Visible Gold Mines closed a C$2.6 million financing to advance its Horsefly project in Quebec, situated directly adjacent to IAMGOLD's 11-million-ounce Nelligan gold deposit. The junior is betting that the same geology hosting Nelligan extends across the property boundary — a classic neighbourhood-play setup in a proven gold camp with a major already in the ground.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD AND SILVER SCORES 4TH MAJOR GALENA DISCOVERY — SIX NEW VEINS WITH 1,392 g/t Ag, 1.5% Cu
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (USAS) announced its fourth major new discovery in one year of drilling at the historic Galena Complex in Idaho, identifying six new high-grade silver-copper-antimony splays returning up to 1,392 g/t Ag, 1.5% Cu and 1.5% Sb over 1.9 metres. The find extends the recently discovered 149 Vein Complex approximately 150 metres to the southwest and underscores what the company calls significant untapped potential across the broader mine corridor.

headline: LUMINA METALS TRIPLES IPO TARGET; C$406M TSX DEBUT LANDS FLAT AS ROSS BEATY'S POLAND COPPER PLAY GETS SIZED UP
snippet: Lumina Metals raised C$406 million at C$12.50 per share in one of 2026's largest mining IPOs on the TSX, selling 25 million shares after the offering was multiple times oversubscribed and the target tripled from the initial C$251 million. Backed by executive chairman Ross Beaty's foundation, the company is developing three copper-silver projects in southwestern Poland and aims to become one of the world's largest copper producers — though shares ended the first trading day essentially flat.

headline: USGS: 2.3 MILLION METRIC TONS OF UNDISCOVERED LITHIUM IN APPALACHIAN ROCK — ENOUGH FOR 328 YEARS OF U.S. IMPORTS
snippet: The U.S. Geological Survey estimated the Appalachian region holds approximately 2.3 million metric tons of economically recoverable lithium in sedimentary brines and hard-rock deposits spread across multiple eastern states — equivalent to 328 years of U.S. imports at last year's consumption rate. The finding is a resource estimate, not a mine-ready inventory; significant capital and permitting hurdles remain before any of it enters production.

headline: MINING M&A HITS $21.6B IN STRONGEST Q1 SINCE 2023; 121 DEALS AS GOLD CONSOLIDATION SEEN AS NEXT FRONTIER
snippet: Global mining mergers and acquisitions totalled $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026 — up 34% from Q1 2025 and the strongest quarterly start since 2023 — according to global law firm White & Case. Precious metals ranked as the sector most likely to see further consolidation, with analysts pointing to record gold prices and still-compressed junior valuations as the conditions needed to drive a new wave of takeouts.

headline: GOLDMAN TURNS TACTICALLY CAUTIOUS ON GOLD; FLAGS HORMUZ DISRUPTIONS AND BOND SELLOFF AS NEAR-TERM RISKS
snippet: Goldman Sachs strategists shifted to a tactically cautious near-term stance on gold, warning that prices remain vulnerable to forced liquidation if Strait of Hormuz disruptions persist or if bond and equity markets come under renewed stress. The bank kept its end-2026 target of $5,400/oz, arguing that structural central bank demand — still running at roughly 60 tonnes per month — provides a durable floor for any dip-buyers.

headline: SILVER HEADING FOR SIXTH STRAIGHT ANNUAL SUPPLY DEFICIT AT 67 MOZ — SOLAR AND EV DEMAND THE CULPRIT
snippet: The silver market is projected to record a 67-million-ounce supply deficit in 2026 — its sixth consecutive annual shortfall — as industrial fabrication demand from solar panel, EV and electronics manufacturing continues to outstrip mine supply and recycling. Since 2021, over 762 million troy ounces have been drawn from global above-ground inventories to cover cumulative deficits, a drawdown that analysts say cannot continue indefinitely without pushing prices higher.

headline: ROYAL CANADIAN MINT HALTS COLOMBIAN GOLD AFTER NYT INVESTIGATION LINKS SUPPLY CHAIN TO DRUG CARTELS
snippet: The Royal Canadian Mint suspended acceptance of Colombian gold and launched a full internal review after a New York Times investigation alleged that gold from Antioquia — a region where the Clan del Golfo cartel controls significant mining operations — was routed through a Texas intermediary, blended with American gold, and presented to the Mint as North American-origin material. The findings raise pointed questions about supply chain due-diligence standards across the broader refining industry.

headline: USA RARE EARTH EYES FURTHER ACQUISITIONS TO DISMANTLE CHINA'S STRANGLEHOLD ON RARE EARTH SUPPLY
snippet: Following its $2.8 billion acquisition of Serra Verde Group's Brazilian rare earth mine and processing plant, USA Rare Earth (USAR) signalled it is actively evaluating additional acquisition targets to build a diversified non-Chinese critical metals supply chain. The stock is up roughly 68% year-to-date as investors bet on a structural realignment of rare earth sourcing driven by U.S. and allied-nation policy pressure on China-dependent supply chains.

headline: GOLDMAN CUTS COPPER FORECAST ON SOFT DEMAND, WARNS PRICES COULD SLIDE DESPITE LONG-TERM GRID TAILWINDS
snippet: Goldman Sachs lowered its near-term copper price forecast, citing softer-than-expected demand and warning that the metal could face further price declines even as long-term electrification tailwinds remain intact. Copper fell to $5.91/lb on May 1, with Chinese sulphuric acid export restrictions adding supply-chain uncertainty for major producers in Chile — the world's top copper-producing nation.
